summary: Matched two small functions in `src/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.c`, raising the file report by 0.43% / 23.36 KB. The PR added wrappers around baselib AX driver behavior: `lbAudioAx_800236B8` keys off an `HSD_SM*` and returns `-1`, while `lbAudioAx_80024B94` clamps an integer argument to ±0x4B0 before forwarding it to `AXDriver_8038D4E4`. It also exposed the needed AX driver prototype in `axdriver.h` and updated scratches entries for the newly accepted matches from `100%` to `OK`. No review discussion was present in the slice.
what_changed: Created/filled `src/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.c` with two matched functions: `lbAudioAx_800236B8` and `lbAudioAx_80024B94`.
`lbAudioAx_80024B94(int arg0, int arg1)` clamps `arg1` to the inclusive range `[-0x4B0, 0x4B0]`, then calls `AXDriver_8038D4E4(arg0, arg1)`.
Added `s32 AXDriver_8038D4E4(int, s16);` to `src/sysdolphin/baselib/axdriver.h` so `lbaudio_ax.c` can call the baselib AX driver helper with the expected narrow second parameter.

Updated `config/GALE01/scratches.txt` so `func_800236B8` and `func_80024B94` are marked `OK` instead of `100%`, reflecting accepted source matches. The slice also moved two `func_someCalcAngle_80022C30` scratch entries from partial-score lines into `OK` lines.
smart_moves: Used the existing baselib AX driver interface instead of reimplementing audio key-off or driver behavior in `lbaudio_ax.c`.
Captured the clamp as a named local constant, `int const limit = 0x4B0`, making the symmetric bounds clear while preserving the constant needed for matching.
Added only the minimal missing prototype to `axdriver.h`, avoiding broader header churn.
The `AXDriver_8038D4E4` prototype uses `s16` for the second parameter, which matches the call pattern after clamping and documents that the driver helper consumes a narrowed signed value.
decomp_lessons: Small wrapper functions can be matched cleanly by identifying the lower-level baselib helper they delegate to and reproducing only argument preparation plus return behavior.
When a function clamps before forwarding to a lower-level routine, preserve the exact clamp bounds and comparison structure; here the relevant bound is `0x4B0` in both directions.
If a matched function depends on a previously undeclared external, add the prototype in the subsystem header (`axdriver.h`) rather than using an ad-hoc local declaration.
After landing matched source, update scratches entries from percentage-based candidates such as `100%` to `OK` so future scratch searches reflect accepted work.
naming_conventions: Newly matched lbaudio functions use the project address-based subsystem style: `lbAudioAx_800236B8` and `lbAudioAx_80024B94`.
Baselib AX driver declarations use the `AXDriver...` prefix, e.g. `AXDriverKeyOff`, `AXDriverUnlink`, and the still-address-named `AXDriver_8038D4E4`.
Scratch entries may retain old raw names such as `func_800236B8` and `func_80024B94` even when the source implementation uses subsystem-prefixed names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: For `lbAudioAx_800236B8`, the match is a direct call to `AXDriverKeyOff` followed by a fixed `-1` return.
For `lbAudioAx_80024B94`, the matching tactic is clamp-low, clamp-high, then tail/normal return through `AXDriver_8038D4E4(arg0, arg1)`.
The constant `0x4B0` is represented as a local `int const limit`, likely helping express both `-limit` and `limit` without duplicating magic numbers.
The header prototype declares the second parameter as `s16`; this is important evidence for the intended argument width at the call boundary.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or document `AXDriver_8038D4E4`.
Check whether more `lbaudio_ax.c` functions follow the same wrapper/clamp pattern around baselib AX driver helpers.
Search for other scratch entries still marked `100%` that can be converted to source plus `OK` entries.
